beats.full_text: If a wave with no warning is familiar, this grief pattern needs a better reset.
beats.ssot_text: If a wave with no warning is familiar, this grief pattern needs a better reset.

beats.speakable_text: If a wave with no warning is familiar, this grief pattern needs a better reset.

beats.full_text: Trying to outrun grief usually makes the loop more expensive. More distraction, more numbing. And less room to actually feel it.
beats.ssot_text: Trying to outrun grief usually makes the loop more expensive: more distraction, more numbing, and less room to actually feel it.
beats.on_screen:
Trying to outrun grief
usually makes the loop

distraction, more numbing.
beats.speakable_text: Trying to outrun grief usually makes the loop more expensive. More distraction, more numbing. And less room to actually feel it.

beats.full_text: Grief often persists because the brain keeps running an old model of the world that still includes the person it lost. Pushing harder can look like progress while the underlying pattern stays the same.
beats.ssot_text: Grief often persists because the brain keeps running an old model of the world that still includes the person it lost. Pushing harder can look like progress while the underlying pattern stays the same.

beats.speakable_text: Grief often persists because the brain keeps running an old model of the world that still includes the person it lost. Pushing harder can look like progress while the underlying pattern stays the same.

beats.full_text: Try this. Name one specific memory out loud, place a hand on your chest. And let the wave pass without rushing it. Keep it small enough to finish before your brain starts negotiating.
beats.ssot_text: Try this: name one specific memory out loud, place a hand on your chest, and let the wave pass without rushing it. Keep it small enough to finish before your brain starts negotiating.

beats.full_text: Bookmark the pattern. Signal, reset, next action. Grief is love with nowhere left to go, not a problem waiting to be solved.
